# WI-QUIC-CRT-0037 - QUIC CRT dormant early-data attempt readiness work item

## Summary

Add a narrow internal readiness observation over the dormant detached resumption carrier while keeping early data explicitly closed and the actual 0-RTT packet family separate.

## Requirements Addressed

- REQ-QUIC-CRT-0140

## Design Inputs

- ARC-QUIC-CRT-0035

## Out of Scope

- 0-RTT packet send/receive support
- anti-replay enforcement
- key update
- public API widening
- IsSupported widening
- broader resumption claims
- broad post-handshake TLS support
- transfer enablement
- retry enablement
- opportunistic refactors unrelated to the new early-data attempt-readiness observation

## Verification Plan

Run focused requirement-home tests for the REQ-QUIC-CRT-0139 prerequisite regression plus positive readiness, missing-prerequisite negative, the public no-broad-0-RTT guard, and the anti-replay/key-update out-of-scope guard; then run the relevant CRT/API/INT guard set, the full `REQ_QUIC_CRT_` sweep, and SpecTrace render/validation checks.

## Completion Notes

The managed client/runtime path now surfaces a narrow internal readiness observation when the dormant detached carrier still has both resumption credential material and early-data prerequisite material, but early data remains explicitly closed and actual 0-RTT packet emission remains a separate family.
